name: Elbmesh Coordinate Phase Work
title: Coordinate Elbmesh Phase Work
summary: Coordinate phased GitHub Issue and PR/MR based Elbmesh delivery, including task cards, agent handoffs, queue state, quality gates, and human decision requests.
description: Use this task for phase ownership and multi-agent coordination rather than individual slice implementation.

validations.title: Phase coordination review
criteria: No implementation is planned outside the active phase or without a GitHub Issue when Issue tracking is in scope.
Parallel tasks are independent and do not conflict on traits/modules.
Every PR/MR has tests or a test plan, named errors, fmt, clippy with warnings denied once configured, cargo test, docs status, and an architecture impact note.
Human decision requests follow docs/HUMAN_DECISION_LOOP.md and include options, recommendation, consequences, and default.

items: Read docs/GOAL.md, docs/GLOSSARY.md, docs/DEVELOPMENT_WORKFLOW.md, docs/HUMAN_DECISION_LOOP.md, docs/PHASED_DELIVERY_PLAN.md, docs/AGENT_SKILLS.md, docs/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md, and relevant ADRs.
Select the active phase, create the next smallest GitHub Issue task card, define acceptance criteria and quality gates, and spawn Test Writer and Implementation Agents only for planned work.
Maintain MR queue entries with phase, issue, pull request, owner agent, dependencies, status, verification result, review result, merge result, follow-up tasks, and human decision requests.
Reject unplanned implementation, speculative abstractions, conflicting parallel work, and unplanned refactors inside feature MRs.
Ask the human only for domain, priority, scope, and architecture blockers covered by docs/HUMAN_DECISION_LOOP.md, using option-based requests.

items.text: Tests for changed behavior are present.
Named errors exist for public/runtime failures.
cargo fmt --check passes or blocker is recorded.
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passes once configured or blocker is recorded.
cargo test --all or justified targeted command passes.
Docs are updated or no-docs-needed is explained.
